offence: Failing to stop after an accident
notes: Leaving the scene of an accident carries 5 to 10 penalty points.

offence: Failing to give particulars or report an accident within 24 hours
notes: Not reporting an accident within 24 hours carries 5 to 10 penalty points.

offence: Undefined accident offences
notes: Other accident-related offences carry 4 to 9 penalty points.

offence: Driving while disqualified by order of court
notes: Driving while already banned carries 6 penalty points.

offence: Attempting to drive while disqualified by order of court
notes: Attempting to drive while banned carries 6 penalty points.

offence: Causing death by driving while disqualified
notes: Causing death while disqualified is a serious offence (3 to 11 points).

offence: Causing serious injury by driving while disqualified
notes: Causing serious injury while disqualified carries 3 to 11 points.

offence: Driving without due care and attention
notes: Careless driving carries 3 to 9 penalty points.

offence: Driving without reasonable consideration for other road users
notes: Inconsiderate driving carries 3 to 9 penalty points.

offence: Driving without due care and attention or without reasonable consideration
notes: Careless or inconsiderate driving carries 3 to 9 penalty points.

offence: Causing serious injury by careless or inconsiderate driving
notes: Causing serious injury by careless driving carries 3 to 11 points.

offence: Causing death through careless driving when unfit through drink
notes: A drink-related death offence; the endorsement stays 11 years from conviction.

offence: Causing death by careless driving when unfit through drugs
notes: A drug-related death offence; the endorsement stays 11 years from conviction.

offence: Causing death by careless driving with alcohol level above the limit
notes: A drink-related death offence; the endorsement stays 11 years from conviction.

offence: Causing death by careless driving then failing to supply a specimen for alcohol analysis
notes: A drink-related death offence; the endorsement stays 11 years from conviction.

offence: Causing death by careless or inconsiderate driving
notes: Causing death by careless driving carries 3 to 11 penalty points.

offence: Causing death by driving: unlicensed disqualified or uninsured drivers
notes: Causing death while unlicensed disqualified or uninsured carries 3 to 11 points.

offence: Using a vehicle with defective brakes
notes: Defective brakes carry 3 penalty points.

offence: Using a vehicle with parts or accessories in a dangerous condition
notes: A dangerously-equipped vehicle carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Using a vehicle with defective tyres
notes: Each defective tyre can carry 3 penalty points.

offence: Using a vehicle with defective steering
notes: Defective steering carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Causing or likely to cause danger by reason of load or passengers
notes: An unsafe load or passengers carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Breach of requirements as to control of the vehicle such as using a mobile phone
notes: Using a hand-held mobile phone while driving carries 6 penalty points and a fine.

offence: Causing serious injury by dangerous driving
notes: Causing serious injury by dangerous driving carries 3 to 11 points.

offence: Dangerous driving
notes: Dangerous driving carries 3 to 11 points and the endorsement stays 4 years from conviction.

offence: Manslaughter or culpable homicide while driving a vehicle
notes: A homicide driving offence carrying 3 to 11 points.

offence: Causing death by dangerous driving
notes: Causing death by dangerous driving carries 3 to 11 points and a long ban.

offence: Furious driving
notes: Furious driving carries 3 to 9 penalty points.

offence: Driving or attempting to drive with alcohol level above the limit
notes: Drink-driving; the endorsement stays on the licence for 11 years from conviction.

offence: Driving or attempting to drive while unfit through drink
notes: Driving while unfit through drink; 11 years from conviction.

offence: Driving or attempting to drive then failing to supply a specimen for analysis
notes: Failing to provide a specimen when driving; 11 years from conviction.

offence: Driving or attempting to drive then refusing analysis of a blood sample taken without consent
notes: Refusing analysis of a blood sample; 11 years from conviction.

offence: In charge of a vehicle while alcohol level above the limit
notes: Being drunk in charge of a vehicle carries 10 penalty points.

offence: In charge of a vehicle while unfit through drink
notes: Being in charge while unfit through drink carries 10 penalty points.

offence: Failure to provide a specimen for analysis (not driving or attempting to drive)
notes: Failing to provide a specimen when in charge carries 10 penalty points.

offence: Refusing analysis of a blood sample taken without consent (not driving)
notes: Refusing analysis when in charge carries 10 penalty points.

offence: Failing to co-operate with a preliminary test
notes: Failing to co-operate with a roadside test carries 4 penalty points.

offence: Driving or attempting to drive with drug level above the specified limit
notes: Drug-driving; the endorsement stays on the licence for 11 years from conviction.

offence: In charge of a vehicle while drug level above the specified limit
notes: Being in charge while over the drug limit carries 10 penalty points.

offence: Causing death by careless driving with drug level above the limit
notes: A drug-related death offence; 11 years from conviction.

offence: Driving or attempting to drive when unfit through drugs
notes: Driving while unfit through drugs; 11 years from conviction.

offence: In charge of a vehicle when unfit through drugs
notes: Being in charge while unfit through drugs carries 10 penalty points.

offence: Using a vehicle uninsured against third-party risks
notes: Driving without insurance carries 6 to 8 penalty points.

offence: Driving otherwise than in accordance with a licence
notes: Driving outside your licence entitlement carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Driving after making a false declaration about fitness when applying for a licence
notes: A false fitness declaration carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Driving a vehicle having failed to notify a disability
notes: Failing to notify a relevant disability carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Driving after a licence has been cancelled or refused on medical grounds
notes: Driving after a medical revocation carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Leaving a vehicle in a dangerous position
notes: Leaving a vehicle dangerously carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Unlawful pillion riding
notes: Unlawful pillion riding carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Play street offences
notes: Play street offences carry 2 penalty points.

offence: Motor racing on the highway
notes: Racing on the highway carries 3 to 11 penalty points.

offence: Offences not covered by other codes
notes: Miscellaneous offences carry around 3 penalty points.

offence: Driving with uncorrected defective eyesight
notes: Driving with defective eyesight carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Refusing to submit to an eyesight test
notes: Refusing an eyesight test carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Failure to give information as to the identity of the driver
notes: Not naming the driver carries 6 penalty points.

offence: Contravention of special roads regulations excluding speed limits
notes: Breaching motorway regulations carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Undefined contravention of pedestrian crossing regulations
notes: A pedestrian-crossing offence carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Contravention of pedestrian crossing regulations with a moving vehicle
notes: A moving-vehicle crossing offence carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Contravention of pedestrian crossing regulations with a stationary vehicle
notes: A stationary-vehicle crossing offence carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Exceeding goods vehicle speed limits
notes: Exceeding a goods-vehicle speed limit carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Exceeding speed limit for type of vehicle excluding goods or passenger vehicles
notes: Exceeding a vehicle-type speed limit carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Exceeding the statutory speed limit on a public road
notes: Speeding on a public road carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Exceeding passenger vehicle speed limit
notes: Exceeding a passenger-vehicle speed limit carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Exceeding the speed limit on a motorway
notes: Speeding on a motorway carries 3 to 6 penalty points.

offence: Failing to comply with traffic-light signals
notes: Running a red light carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Failing to comply with double white lines
notes: Crossing double white lines carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Failing to comply with a stop sign
notes: Ignoring a stop sign carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Failing to comply with the direction of a constable or warden
notes: Ignoring a constable's direction carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Failing to comply with a traffic sign excluding stop signs traffic lights or double white lines
notes: Ignoring a traffic sign carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Failing to comply with a school crossing patrol sign
notes: Ignoring a school crossing patrol carries 3 penalty points.

offence: Undefined failure to comply with a traffic direction sign
notes: Other traffic-sign offences carry 3 penalty points.

offence: Aggravated taking of a vehicle
notes: Aggravated vehicle taking carries 3 to 11 penalty points.

offence: Disqualification under totting-up
notes: Shown on the record when a driver is disqualified for reaching 12 or more points within 3 years.

offence: Totting-up disqualification rule
notes: A driver who totals 12 or more penalty points within 3 years is disqualified for at least 6 months.

offence: New driver revocation rule
notes: A driver who reaches 6 or more penalty points within 2 years of passing their first test has their licence revoked and must re-apply and re-pass both theory and practical tests.

offence: Probationary period for new drivers
notes: The first 2 years after passing the first driving test are probationary; the 6-point revocation rule applies during this period.

offence: Minimum totting-up disqualification period
notes: A totting-up ban is normally at least 6 months; 12 months if disqualified once in the previous 3 years and 2 years if disqualified twice in the previous 3 years.

offence: Exceptional hardship plea
notes: A driver facing a totting-up ban may argue exceptional hardship to avoid disqualification; it must go beyond ordinary inconvenience and is decided by the court and cannot reuse the same grounds within 3 years.

offence: How long points stay on the licence
notes: Most endorsements stay 4 years from the date of the offence; dangerous driving stays 4 years from conviction; serious drink or drug and causing-death offences stay 11 years from conviction.
